vitals.location: Knysna Waterfront (Oyster Festival Grounds), Garden Route, Western Cape, South Africa
vitals.field_size: 183 classified finishers (2026 long route); 2,000+ across the full road/MTB/family weekend
vitals.cutoff_time: Barrington Hill checkpoint cutoff (no published clock time)

climate.description: Winter on South Africa's southern Cape coast (mid-July) — no organizer-published weather or wind data for this specific route.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 57. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 54. EDITORIAL WAVE 10 FLAG (2026-07-24): prior course_description and youtube_data described a ~130km remote gravel ride over Prince Alfred's Pass (Cape Epic-adjacent terrain) — that is a different, unrelated ride, not this paved 102km road event. Corrected this wave. Dimension scores below were inherited from the pre-correction profile and have NOT been independently re-evaluated against the corrected identity (a flat-to-rolling paved coastal road race is a materially different difficulty profile than a remote gravel mountain-pass ride) — flagging for human rescoring rather than silently changing numbers. No numeric changes made this wave.

course_description.character: A paved coastal road race run out of the Knysna Oyster Festival grounds on the Garden Route. The 102km long route opens with about 6km of flat, fast riding through town, then climbs the roughly 2.5km Kyters Nek before heading out toward Buffalo Bay, where two steeper coastal climbs follow. From there the route passes Groen Vlei toward Sedgefield before turning back for the long Barrington Hill climb and an undulating run back into Knysna on the N2. None of this touches Prince Alfred's Pass or the Seven Passes gravel road — those are a separate, much longer backcountry ride roughly 90 minutes north that has nothing to do with this race (see catalog_flags).
course_description.signature_challenge: Barrington Hill — the longest, latest climb on the course, after riders are already worked from Kyters Nek and the Buffalo Bay climbs.
suffering_zones: Kyters Nek (~2.5km climb early in the route, right after the flat opening)
The two steeper coastal climbs around Buffalo Bay
Barrington Hill — the longest climb on the course, late in the route

history.origin_story: Started in 1987 as a Knysna tourism initiative to draw winter visitors to the town; the Rotary Club of Knysna took full control of the race in 1993-94 and moved it into a trust structure in 2006-07 to manage liability and tax exposure. It's now run as an anchor cycling event of the annual Knysna Oyster Festival.
history.reputation: An anchor cycling event of the Knysna Oyster Festival.

biased_opinion.verdict: A Modest, Founded-1987 Garden Route Fixture
biased_opinion.summary: The Knysna Cycle Tour is a paved 102km road race that fills out Oyster Festival weekend on the Garden Route: a flat opening, a handful of coastal climbs, and a genuine sting in Barrington Hill late on. The organizer publishes GPX files but no ascent total, no long-route water-point count, and no fixed cutoff clock time — thin pre-race information for a traveling rider to plan around. The research dump documents one 2016 taxi incident that entered the cycling lane; current entry terms allow no refunds even for organizer-side route changes or cancellation.
strengths: Founded in 1987 and established as an anchor cycling event of the Oyster Festival
Barrington Hill is a real, late climb that rewards riders who paced the earlier coastal climbs
Garden Route coastal roads through Buffalo Bay and Sedgefield
Rotary Club organization with a documented multi-decade track record
weaknesses: No published ascent total, long-route feed-zone count, or fixed cutoff time — hard to plan pacing or logistics around
Field size is modest (183 classified finishers in the 2026 long route) versus 2,000+ across the whole festival weekend
No refunds even for organizer-driven postponement, route changes, or weather cancellation
The research dump documents a 2016 taxi incident (entered the cycling lane, minor injuries)
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A Garden Route road race worth riding if you're already in Knysna for Oyster Festival weekend — go in expecting a modest field and thin pre-race course documentation.

final_verdict.one_liner: A modest, founded-1987 Garden Route road race built around Oyster Festival weekend.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Worth it if you're in Knysna for Oyster Festival weekend anyway and want a paved road race with one real late climb (Barrington Hill). Not worth a dedicated trip on its own merits — field size and course information are both modest.
final_verdict.alternatives: For another South African road event: Cape Town Cycle Tour (109km, Cape Town). For the actual gravel Prince Alfred's Pass experience: Lions Karoo to Coast (Uniondale to Knysna, ~96km off-road, held separately in September).

eligibility.notes: 2026 edition ran 12 July 2026 (event weekend 10-12 July: Fri registration, Sat MTB/family, Sun 102km/42km road races at 08:00) and produced official BoutTime results (183 classified finishers, long route). Entries showing as 'closed' reflect the 2026 edition having already run, not cancellation. Distance corrected 99.8km->102km (organizer-advertised; BoutTime pace data implies ~100.0km measured). Elevation, long-route feed zones, and a fixed cutoff clock time are not published by the organizer for the 2026 route — set to null/honest language rather than carrying forward the unverifiable 1,500m/6hr figures. via codex gpt-5.6-sol web research, editorial wave 10.

catalog_flags.status_note: Identity-corrected this wave (2026-07-24): the prior course_description and all youtube_data videos/quotes/rider_intel described a ~130km remote gravel ride over Prince Alfred's Pass and the Seven Passes road (Cape Epic-adjacent backcountry terrain, documented in a first-person CADE Media video and a Prince Alfred's Pass motorbike vlog) — an entirely different, unrelated ride roughly 90 minutes from Knysna. This is a paved 102km road race run out of the Oyster Festival grounds. All youtube_data removed as non-matching; none of the five videos on file (gravel ride vlog, motorbike pass tour, an unidentified 'SA Cycling Elite Men Race' clip, a 19.7km FULGAZ virtual-ride recording, and 'Swartberg Gran Fondo 2018' — a different race entirely) verifiably depicts this event.
